title: Atlassian Design System — Voice and Tone Principles
insights: Voice as 'a colleague who is always up on trends'. Motivate teams, offer best practices, be bold. Empathetic teacher model. Get in and get out — don't over-explain.

title: Atlassian — Writing Guidelines
insights: Covers language, grammar, inclusive language, vocabulary. Clear + concise + conversational triple principle.

title: IBM Carbon Design System — Content Overview
insights: Voice: simple, logical, intellectually ambitious, persuasive not poetic, confident not boastful. Same voice always; tone adapts per situation.

title: Unicode CLDR Project — Common Locale Data Repository
insights: Canonical source for locale-specific patterns: dates, numbers, currencies, plural rules, timezones. Powers all major i18n libraries. Plural category definitions (zero/one/two/few/many/other) defined here.

title: Intuit Content Design System — Voice and Tone
insights: Voice inspires customers toward prosperity. Strip hyperbolic language and upsells. Transparent in complex situations. Product-specific voice per QuickBooks/TurboTax.

title: GOV.UK Design System — Styles (including writing guidance)
insights: Plain English mandatory. Active voice, short sentences, subheadings, simple vocabulary. Title max 65 characters. Research-backed rules.

title: Apple HIG — Writing
insights: Words are part of UX. Plain language, accessibility-first, localization-aware. Small screens require extreme brevity.

title: Microsoft Fluent 2 — Content Design
insights: Warm, relaxed, crisp, clear — three-adjective voice. Second person (you/your). Sentence-style capitalization. Error messages: warm not blaming, tells what happened + what's next + realistic solution.

title: FormatJS — ICU Syntax Reference
insights: Canonical ICU MessageFormat reference for React ecosystem. Plural/select/selectordinal/number/date. ICU plural rules in message not JSX logic. Safest pluralization strategy for all languages.

title: FormatJS react-intl — Full Documentation
insights: Most widely used ICU implementation in JavaScript. Supports all argument types, number skeletons, nested expressions. ESM artifacts. Node 16+ required. @formatjs/icu-messageformat-parser underlies everything.

title: Material Design 3 — Content Design Overview
insights: Sentence-case capitalization for all UI elements. Second person preferred. Focus on user action not app action. Voice consistent, tone contextual.

title: Polaris Actionable Language — verb+noun formula for buttons
insights: Buttons use {verb}+{noun} except common actions (Done, Close, Cancel). Lead with strong verb. Never 'Click here'. Start sentences with imperative verbs — not 'You can'.

title: Polaris Grammar and Mechanics — sentence case everywhere
insights: Sentence case universally — never title case in UI. First word + proper nouns only. Prevents inconsistency at scale.

title: Polaris — Actionable language foundations
insights: Menu actions follow verb+noun. If context is clear, verb alone suffices. Avoid permissive 'you can' — use imperative directly.

title: Adobe Spectrum — Inclusive UX Writing
insights: Language without visuals test: write as if images don't exist. Binary gender assumptions must be avoided.

title: Mailchimp Content Style Guide — Home (CC BY-NC 4.0)
insights: Publicly licensed style guide covering grammar, web, social, email, legal, accessibility, translation. Gold standard for open-source content guides.

title: Mailchimp Content Style Guide — Voice and Tone
insights: Voice is constant; tone adapts to reader's emotional state. Informal but clarity beats entertainment. Sense of humor is permitted, forced humor is not. Active voice. Avoid jargon.

title: GOV.UK — Writing for GOV.UK guidance
insights: Content design methodology originated here. User need over organizational preference. Research-first before writing.

title: Nielsen Norman Group — The 3 I's of Microcopy
insights: Microcopy must Inform, Influence, and support Interaction. Fewer than 3 sentences. Four tone dimensions: humor, formality, respectfulness, enthusiasm.

title: NNG — 10 Design Guidelines for Form Error Reporting
insights: Inline validation, error near field, red + icon (never color alone). On blur not on keypress. Explicit, human-readable, polite, precise, constructive.

title: NNG — Placeholders in Form Fields Are Harmful
insights: Placeholder as label is harmful. Always use visible persistent labels. Placeholder = hint/example only.

title: NNG — Tooltip Guidelines
insights: Tooltips: supplemental info for non-critical context. 3 lines max. Hover-only = keyboard inaccessible. No links inside.

title: Unicode Technical Report TR35 — Numbers and Currencies
insights: Formal specification for number/currency format patterns. Symbols (#, ,, .) are placeholders not literals — locale substitutes actual separators. Standard and accounting currency formats. Basis for Intl.NumberFormat.

title: Adrian Roselli — More Accessible Skeletons
insights: Skeleton screens: screen reader must announce loading state. aria-live or aria-busy for in-progress states. Left-to-right shimmer direction (not vertical). Mirrors final layout exactly to prevent CLS. Pulse vs shimmer preference.

title: Slack — Voice and Tone: communicating for clarity
insights: Clear, concise, human — 'friendly intelligent coworker'. Foreground task information first. Empathy toward every user.

title: Atlassian — Writing Style
insights: Speaks its mind quickly. Simplifies complex problems. Confident but not boastful. Persuasive not poetic.

title: Baymard — 5 Proven UX Strategies for No-Results Pages
insights: ~50% of sites fail no-results recovery. Show query, suggest spelling fix, offer related categories, never a dead end. DuckDuckGo pattern: [query] + 4 actionable recovery steps.

title: IBM Carbon — Writing Style (sentence case)
insights: Sentence case for all UI elements. Research validates faster reading. IBM standard.

title: Google Codelabs — Material's Communication Principles: Intro to UX Writing
insights: Hands-on codelab for UX writing with Material Design. Short scannable text, second person, sentence case, no periods on labels. Focus on user action not system action.

title: Intuit Content Design — Confirmations
insights: Avoid 'Are you sure?' — specify what 'this' is. Button labels as binary dichotomous verbs. 'Delete file' / 'Keep file' over Yes/No.

title: Intuit Content Design — Fields
insights: Placeholder text supplements labels, never replaces them. Realistic examples prevent formatting errors.

title: Intuit Content Design — Tooltips
insights: Headers 60 chars max, body 130 chars max. Lead with verb introducing function. No links inside. Keyboard accessible.

title: Cookie Information — French Cookie Guidelines (CNIL)
insights: Accept and Reject buttons must have equal prominence — CNIL fined companies for multi-click reject vs one-click accept. Explicit consent required for non-essential. Cookie wall = non-compliant. Withdrawal as easy as consent. No dark patterns.

title: Crowdin Blog — ICU Message Format Guide (2026)
insights: Most current ICU guide. String management: clear descriptive keys like 'cta_buy_now'. Context metadata helps translators. Continuous localization via CI/CD. String comments for translator guidance.

title: Crowdin — Software Localization Advanced Guide for Product Teams
insights: Externalize strings from first commit. Treat content as data. CI/CD integration: auto-sync on push. Continuous localization vs batch. String context metadata = translator quality improvement. Descriptive key naming conventions.

title: CXL — Microcopy: Tiny Words That Make A Huge Impact on Conversions
insights: 'Request pricing' vs 'Request a quote' = +161.66% clicks. 'We'll never spam you' placeholder = +15% form completions. 'Shop Medicare Plans' vs 'Get started now' = +192% clicks. Evidence base for ROI of content design.

title: i18next/react-i18next GitHub Repository
insights: Most popular React i18n library. Hooks-based (useTranslation). Integrates with Lokalise/Phrase/Crowdin. Lazy namespace loading. Browser language detection. Next.js plugin (next-i18next).

title: Lingui/js-lingui GitHub Repository (1K+ stars)
insights: Readable, automated, optimized (2KB) i18n. Babel macros for compile-time extraction. PO format for professional translators. Strict TypeScript. ICU under the hood. CLI extracts, compiles, validates. RSC support.

title: Mailchimp content-style-guide GitHub source — voice-and-tone.md
insights: Raw source for Mailchimp's published guide. Useful for forking or adapting. Confirms the positive language rule: reframe negatives as guidance not blame.

title: 18F Content Guide — Plain Language approach
insights: Write one-on-one as if helping. Buy not purchase, help not assist. Explain technical terms immediately. Based on GOV.UK + AP Stylebook + PlainLanguage.gov. Federal-grade standard.

title: IxDF — Progressive Disclosure (2026 updated)
insights: Defers advanced features to secondary UI. Reduces cognitive load. Aligns with information architecture hierarchy. Copy on controls must have information scent strong enough to trigger correct disclosure path.

title: Microsoft Writing Style Guide
insights: Comprehensive public style guide: capitalization, bias-free communication, numbers, contractions, punctuation. Pairs with Fluent design voice.

title: Lingui 5.0 — Release Announcement
insights: Lingui 5: new useLingui macro simplifies non-JSX i18n in React. PO file comments show placeholder context for translators. ICU under the hood. 2KB runtime. React Server Components supported. Vite plugin + CLI.

title: Lokalise — Complete Guide to ICU Message Format
insights: ICU pluralization powered by CLDR. Upload files with 'Detect ICU plurals' enabled. Plural categories: zero, one, two, few, many, other — language-specific. CLDR defines rules per locale.

title: Lokalise — React i18n with react-intl Step-by-Step
insights: i18n provider pattern: put locale state in one provider, components stay unaware. Descriptive keys (nav.home not full sentence). Use placeholders {count}, {date}, never concatenate.

title: Material Design 3 — Style Guide Word Choice
insights: Avoid periods and unnecessary punctuation on labels. Numerals over spelled-out numbers for counts. Conversational friendly global-audience writing.

title: next-intl — App Router Internationalization for Next.js
insights: Top [locale] dynamic segment pattern. Prefix-based (/fr/about) or domain-based routing. Built-in support for date/number formatting. RSC-compatible. De facto standard for Next.js App Router i18n.

title: Phrase — Practical Guide to ICU Message Format
insights: select for gender/role, plural for counts, selectordinal for rankings. Nested ICU for complex sentences. Never break strings mid-sentence for variables — context lost for translators.

title: PlainLanguage.gov — Federal Plain Language Guidelines
insights: Mandated by Plain Writing Act 2010. Use 'you', active voice, short words, short sentences. Canonical reference for government-grade readability.

title: react-i18next — Using with ICU Format
insights: Official ICU extension for i18next. Typed with TypeScript. Strict mode compatible. Supports number, date, time, select, plural, selectOrdinal. ICU macro provides JSX-compatible syntax. Plugin pattern — add i18next-icu to options.

title: Adobe Spectrum — Grammar and Mechanics
insights: Comprehensive grammar rules for UI copy across all Adobe Clouds. Emphasis on visual hierarchy through typography.

title: Adobe Spectrum — Writing for Onboarding
insights: Onboarding copy introduces features as signposts. Consistent terminology critical. Clear verbs over vague ones.

title: UX Content Collective — International Guide to Gender-Inclusive Writing
insights: Language-by-language strategies. French: élu·es dot format (contested), avoid gendered profession nouns, restructure sentences. They/them in English as default. Ask users pronoun preference at signup. Variables for gender-inflected interfaces.

title: Content Design (book) — Sarah Winters
insights: Foundational text for the discipline. GDS methodology: user need first, data-driven content decisions, format follows need (infographic/video/text/table). Step-by-step process from user research to published content.

title: NNG — Progressive Disclosure
insights: Defers advanced features to secondary UI. Reduces cognitive load. Accordion/tabs/dialogs as implementation patterns.

title: NNG — UX Writing Study Guide
insights: Comprehensive research-backed curriculum for UX writing. Canonical NNG resource hub.

title: Smashing Magazine — Designing Better Error Messages UX
insights: Error placement above input fields. Toast errors usually wrong choice. Error voice reveals product's overall voice.

title: Smashing Magazine — Complete Guide To Live Validation UX
insights: Inline validation reduces errors significantly. Validate on blur not on keyup. Positive validation builds confidence.

title: Smashing Magazine — How to Improve Microcopy (2024)
insights: 2024 edition. Empathy-first approach to error copy. AI-assisted draft generation now common but needs human voice review. Compassionate words in error states ('Oops! That password doesn't match. Need help resetting it?').

title: Beth Aitman — How to Write a Confirmation Dialog
insights: Specific action in headline: 'Delete invoice #4839' not 'Delete invoice'. Match button text to preceding selection.

title: Hemingway Editor — Readability Checker
insights: Grade 7-8 reading level target for maximum reach. Flags: long sentences, passive voice, adverbs, complex words. Run UI labels, error messages, placeholder text through Hemingway for baseline quality check.

title: LingoHub — French Localization Peculiarities and Challenges
insights: French 15-20% longer than English. Possessive pronouns reflect noun gender not person gender. Adjectives inflect. Never split noun from possessive across string variables. Inclusive writing: élu·es dot format for gender-neutral.

title: Pseudo-localization for Automated i18n Testing (Anton Antonov)
insights: Replaces Latin with accented/extended chars. Wraps text with markers for clipping detection. Catches German/Finnish ~35% expansion, Arabic 20-25%, French 15-20%. Use early — before translations start.

title: Shopify Voice and Tone Guide (Biz Sanford, Shopify Design)
insights: Shopify voice: direct, human, merchant-first. Tone shifts by context. Moved away from assuming audience emotions — frame around product situation instead.

title: Phrase — 10 Common Mistakes in Software Localization
insights: Top mistakes: hardcoded strings, string concatenation (breaks in Japanese), pluralization via code not ICU, ignoring text expansion, no context for translators, pseudo-locale testing skipped.

title: SimpleLocalize — ICU Message Format: Guide to Plurals, Dates, Localization Syntax
insights: Practical ICU examples: {count, plural, one{# item} other{# items}}. Nested ICU: {gender, select, male{He} female{She} other{They}} combined with count. Date/time skeleton patterns. Accessible to developers new to i18n.

title: Slack Design — 5 Principles for Marketing Copy
insights: Brand voice as editorial identity. One voice, multiple tones. Cultural sensitivity for global audience.

title: UX Content Collective — What is Content Design?
insights: Content design coined by Sarah Winters at GDS UK. UX writing = sub-discipline of content design. Meta/Shopify/Netflix/Microsoft shifted title from 'UX writer' to 'content designer' mid-2010s. Strategic, cross-platform discipline vs reactive microcopy.

title: UX Collective — 'Are you sure?' Microcopy for Confirmation Dialogues
insights: 'Are you sure?' is useless. Buttons must summarize outcome. 'Delete file' / 'Keep file'. Undo pattern beats confirmation for reversible actions.

title: ECTOS — Tu vs Vous in French: Grammar of Leadership
insights: Vous: traditional, respectful, professional distance — use for legal/finance/institutional HR software. Tu: warm, collaborative — startups and creative environments. This is a strategic brand-culture decision for every French-language product.

title: French Business Advice — GDPR and French Social Law
insights: HR-specific: French employers cannot rely on employee consent for PII processing — power imbalance makes consent non-free. Legitimate interest or legal obligation must be the basis. UI copy for HR data processing must NOT use consent language for mandatory payroll data.

title: Salesforce Lightning Design System 2 — Voice and Tone
insights: Voice guidelines for making Salesforce content clear, consistent, appealing. Business-enterprise tone with warmth.

title: PatternFly — Capitalization UX Writing
insights: Sentence case for all titles, headings, subtitles, subheadings. Consistency argument: title case requires complex rules that differ person-to-person. Sentence case scales.

title: Smashing Magazine — Right-To-Left Development in Mobile Design
insights: RTL is not mirror. Icons must be logically mirrored not literally. Numbers/emails/URLs stay LTR in RTL context. Arabic 20-25% expansion. Hebrew lacks uppercase. Font size +1-2pt for RTL to match visual weight. CSS logical properties over physical.

title: GitHub Docs Style Guide
insights: Active voice mandatory. No region-specific idioms. Anti-racist and accessible word choices.

title: Vercel Web Interface Guidelines — GitHub repo
insights: Guidelines as code: versioned, open, forkable. Covers writing, layout, performance, accessibility.

title: Vercel Web Interface Guidelines
insights: Headings + buttons: Title Case. Marketing: sentence case. Active voice. Numerals for counts. Skeletons mirror final content exactly.

title: LogRocket — Designing 'No Results Found' Pages
insights: Generic 'no results found' = conversion blocker. Show query back to user. Offer spelling corrections, related content, category browse. 50% of sites create UX dead ends here.

title: Microsoft Design — The Microsoft Voice, Part 1: You Had Me at Human
insights: Microsoft voice evolution: corporate to human. Research-backed: people respond to friendly, helpful, concise. The 'warm and relaxed' principle. Conversational one-on-one framing.

title: How Stripe Built a Writing Culture
insights: Stripe standardizes through sample docs not rigid templates. Stripe Press and Increment: writing excellence as company differentiator.

title: UX Writing Hub — Empty State Examples and Guidelines
insights: Two parts instruction, one part delight. Headline + description + CTA structure. Never imply user fault.

title: UX Writing Hub — Error Message Examples and Guidelines
insights: Single source of truth prevents multiple variants of same error. Document rules with examples.

title: NZ Digital Government — Gender-Inclusive Language Guide
insights: Use they/them as default pronoun for hypothetical persons. Restructure to avoid gendered pronouns before adding singular they. Government-grade authoritative guidance applicable to enterprise HR software.

title: Pencil & Paper — Empty State UX Examples
insights: Structured approach: headline + motivation copy + CTA. No results search state needs next steps not dead end.

title: Built In — 16 Rules of Effective UX Writing
insights: Research summary of cross-source rules. Clarity, concision, consistency triad. One idea per label. Read aloud test. User-centrism over brand voice in conflict.

title: Stripe Apps — Design Your App guidelines
insights: Polaris-based design system for Stripe apps. Content-first approach. Writing guidelines tied to component usage.

title: Bootcamp — UX Writing for Onboarding
insights: Signpost model: copy as navigation through feature introduction. Break journey into steps with distinct information per step. Consistent term usage prevents cognitive friction (Meeting ID vs Meeting Code).

title: Secure Privacy — GDPR Compliance: CNIL Cookie Guidelines
insights: CNIL stricter than most EU regulators. Fines for disproportionate accept UX. Granular controls required per purpose. Banner must name purposes, owners, rejection right, link to privacy policy.

title: Airbnb Tone of Voice Analysis
insights: Airbnb voice: straightforward, inclusive, thoughtful, spirited. 27 languages. Storytelling + belonging framing.

title: Every Interaction — Title Case vs Sentence Case in UI
insights: Apple uses title case for buttons/menus; Google/Carbon/PatternFly use sentence case. Research: sentence case faster to read for longer labels. Title case: harder to explain consistently to large teams. Pick one at system level.

title: Manatal — Crafting Rejection Emails That Leave a Positive Impression
insights: Candidates who have a positive rejection experience are 3.5x more likely to apply again. Rejection is a brand-building event. Personalization scales with interview depth. Appreciate effort, be clear, avoid vague apologies.

title: UX Writing Toasts — Ultimate Writing Guide
insights: Toasts: short statements not sentences. Fragments acceptable: 'Note copied'. Generic over specific reduces variants. Error toasts are problematic — use banners for persistent errors.
